purpose:
Apply strengthened Belinda 3Q audit on any candidate that has passed Phases 2 + 3. Strict audit requires:
  - Q1: atom must contain >=1 mechanism-vocabulary word (attention, gradient, optimization, sampling, embedding, training, inference, residual, normalization, routing) — NOT filler (framework, principle, axis, substrate).
  - Q2: operator must be PREDICTION_RESOLVES_BLOCKER or BLOCKER_DISSOLVED_BY_PRINCIPLE or PREDICTION_GROUNDED_IN_PRINCIPLE. REJECT 'ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN' (too easy).
  - Q3: verbatim quote >= 30 characters AND not pure filler/meta-talk.
input_candidates: 0 from Run 11 recheck (all 24 failed Phase 2 threshold 0.90)

demonstration_audit_on_run_11_top_candid.phase_4_status: Not actually reached, but for diagnostic clarity I apply strict audit:

Q1_mechanism_vocabulary.atom_a_text: Architecture and training rules are fundamentally the SAME thing — different levels of optimization, each with its own context flow and update rate. The duality between design-time and run-time vanishes.
Q1_mechanism_vocabulary.atom_b_text: A full GPT (tokenizer, autograd, transformer, Adam, training loop, inference) fits in 200 lines of dependency-free Python.
Q1_mechanism_vocabulary.Q1_verdict: PASS_MARGINAL (mechanism words present in both atoms, but joint_topic and novel_claim lean on filler vocabulary 'principle' and 'substrate' — would benefit from explicit operator)

Q2_operator_check.candidate_operator: ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN (compression-principle from microGPT analogy-transferred to Nested Learning's design-time/run-time unification)
Q2_operator_check.Q2_verdict: REJECT_BY_STRICT_RULE (ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN is exactly the rejected operator). The candidate is a surface analogy, not a prediction-resolves-blocker or principle-dissolves-blocker case.
Q2_operator_check.operator_alternative_that_would_have_pas: If the candidate had said 'Nested Learning's update-rate hierarchy RESOLVES the BLOCKER that microGPT cannot represent its own training loop within 200 lines because the loop is the same level as the architecture' — that would have been BLOCKER_DISSOLVED_BY_PRINCIPLE. As stated, it is just analogy.

Q3_verbatim_quote.candidate_quote: Architecture and training rules are fundamentally the SAME thing — different levels of optimization, each with its own context flow and update rate.

demonstration_audit_on_run_11_top_candid.final_strict_verdict: REJECT (Q2 fails: operator is ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OPEN, which is rejected by strict rule)

operator_distribution_in_run_11_24_survi.estimation_basis: All Run 11 survivors use construction template 'X concept as Y concept analog' or 'X is the substrate for Y'. Almost none explicitly identify a BLOCKER and dissolve it by a PRINCIPLE. The 2 marginal exceptions are CAND_011_E4_007 (hallucination_reduction as info-thermodynamic constraint minimization — may be PREDICTION_GROUNDED_IN_PRINCIPLE) and CAND_011_E2_002 (verifier_density_gradient as selection_pressure — may be PREDICTION_GROUNDED_IN_PRINCIPLE). Both are quarantined though.
operator_distribution_in_run_11_24_survi.implication: If Phase 4 strict-Belinda were applied alone to Run 11's 24 survivors (ignoring Phases 2-3), the survivor count would drop from 24 to <=2. Combined with Phases 2+3, count drops to 0.

summary.n_rejected_by_Q2_ANALOGY_TRANSFERS_TO_OP: n/a (gate not reached); estimated 22/24 if applied alone
summary.n_rejected_by_Q3_short_quote: n/a (most Run 11 quotes are >30 chars, so Q3 alone is not the binding constraint)

tool_audit_phase_4.status: PASS (no web_search required for syntactic audit)
